description_long: This crustacean object features a robust, segmented body with a pronounced cephalothorax and a rounded abdomen. Its two large, prominent claws extend outward from the front, flanking a head adorned with a pair of long, curved antennae. The legs are thick and jointed, with four visible on each side, giving the creature a sturdy, balanced stance. The eyes are positioned on the head, appearing as small, rounded protrusions. The surface texture appears hard and slightly rough, typical of a crustacean exoskeleton. The overall form is compact and lifelike, with anatomical details that suggest a lobster or crab-like species.
description: A robust, segmented crustacean with large claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured exoskeleton. Its body is compact and lifelike, with detailed limbs and a rounded abdomen.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large claws, the head with antennae, and the symmetrical segmentation of the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the body, the curvature of the antennae, and the layered segmentation of the abdomen.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded abdomen, the arrangement of the legs, and the symmetry of the body segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the other profile of the claws, antennae, and body depth.
